channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Tier 1 geopolitical news headlines curated by Frontion

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳 Trump Lands in Beijing for High-Stakes Summit with Xi Jinping
item.description: US President Donald Trump arrived in China for his first bilateral meeting hosted there in nearly a decade, accompanied by tech leaders including Elon Musk and Jensen Huang. The Iran war and its impact on global energy markets loom over talks on trade, Taiwan, and AI.

item.title: 🏀🏳️‍🌈 Jason Collins, NBA First Openly Gay Player, Dies at 47
item.description: Jason Collins, who made history in 2013 as the NBA first openly gay player, has died from brain cancer at age 47. Collins spent 13 seasons in the league and became a pioneer for inclusion in professional sports.

item.title: 🇫🇷🇰🇪🌍 Macron Announces 27 Billion in New African Investments
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron announced 23 billion euros in investments in Africa at a summit in Kenya, with 14 billion from French companies and 9 billion from African entities. The Africa Forward Summit marks a shift toward sovereign equality in France-Africa relations.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳🇮🇷 Trump Arrives in Beijing as Iran War Casts Shadow Over China Summit
item.description: Trump downplayed Chinas potential role in ending the Iran conflict, saying the US does not need help. Trade tensions, Taiwan, AI, and rare earth exports dominate the agenda as the Iran war and Strait of Hormuz disruptions add urgency to the summit.

item.title: 🇺🇦⚔️ Russia Bombards Ukraine with 100+ Drones as Trump Talks Peace
item.description: More than 100 Russian drones targeted Ukraine in the latest wave of strikes, hitting railway infrastructure, civilian sites, ports, and energy facilities. At least eight people were killed in Tuesdays barrage as Zelenskyy urged continued international support.

item.title: 🇪🇺 NATO Leaders Call for Strengthened Air Defences After Russian Airspace Breaches
item.description: Leaders from 14 NATO countries on the eastern flank urged the alliance to consolidate air defences against missiles and drones following repeated Russian airspace violations.

item.title: ⛽ EU Gas Reliance on US Raises Supply Risk Concerns
item.description: European energy regulators warned that the EU's growing dependence on U.S. liquefied natural gas carries risks, including rising dependence on a single supplier.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳 US LNG Vessels Resume China Shipments After Year-Long Pause Ahead of Trump-Xi Summit
item.description: China may receive its first direct U.S. liquefied natural gas shipment in over a year, signaling thawing energy ties as Trump heads to Beijing for talks with Xi Jinping.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Post-War Gaza Envoy Visits Jerusalem to Advance Ceasefire Deal
item.description: The top diplomat overseeing the U.S.-brokered Gaza ceasefire deal is in Jerusalem to advance implementation of the agreement.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳 Trump Arrives in Beijing for Xi Summit With Nvidia CEO and Musk in Tow
item.description: President Trump landed in China with a high-profile business delegation including Nvidia's Jensen Huang and Elon Musk, seeking to 'open up' China to U.S. business amid ongoing Iran war tensions. The summit marks the first U.S. presidential visit to China in nearly a decade.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Russian Black Sea oil terminal ablaze after Ukrainian drone strikes
item.description: Ukrainian forces struck the Tamanneftegaz oil terminal in Krasnodar Krai, as well as facilities in Yaroslavl and Astrakhan, causing significant damage to Russian energy export infrastructure on the same day Moscow celebrated its Navy anniversary.

item.title: 🤖 Ukrainian forces deploy ground robot to destroy Russian-held building
item.description: Ukraines 100th Mechanized Brigade used a kamikaze ground robot to attack a building occupied by Russian sabotage troops in Kostiantynivka, demonstrating evolving robotic warfare tactics on the battlefield.

item.title: 🇵🇭 Gunfire erupts in Philippine Senate during ICC arrest attempt of senator
item.description: Shots were fired inside the Philippine Senate as authorities attempted to arrest Senator Ronald dela Rosa, wanted by the International Criminal Court for crimes against humanity related to the Duterte drug war. No casualties were reported.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇹🇼 China Warns US on Taiwan Arms Sales Ahead of Trump-Xi Summit
item.description: Beijing reiterated strong opposition to U.S. arms sales to Taiwan just hours before Trump's arrival, calling on Washington to honor its commitments. The warning underscores the tensions that will loom over the high-stakes summit.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Corruption Charges Hit Zelenskiy's Inner Circle as Ukraine War Drags On
item.description: After years of graft allegations swirling around his associates, President Zelenskiy now faces a bigger test with corruption charges reaching his former right-hand man. The case could weaken Ukraine's wartime leadership at a critical juncture.

item.title: 💰 US and China Weigh Tariff Cuts on $30 Billion of Imports
item.description: Washington and Beijing are expected to move toward a managed trade mechanism, with each side potentially identifying $30 billion in goods for tariff reductions. The deal would avoid national security red lines while easing trade tensions.

item.title: 🇵🇭 Gunshots at Philippine Senate as Police Standoff Escalates
item.description: More than a dozen gunshots were heard at the Philippine Senate during a standoff involving a senator with an outstanding arrest warrant. The building was placed on lockdown with personnel told to take cover.

item.title: 🇬🇧 Britain to Strengthen EU Ties in New Legislative Agenda
item.description: King Charles delivered the governments legislative agenda including legislation to strengthen ties with the European Union, as Prime Minister Keir Starmer fights to stay in power amid leadership pressure.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israel Escalates Gaza Strikes as Hamas Regroups After Iran Truce
item.description: Israel has intensified attacks on Gaza in the five weeks since halting joint bombing with the US in Iran, redirecting fire back to the Palestinian enclave where Hamas fighters are tightening their grip.

item.title: 🇯🇵 OECD Projects Bank of Japan to Raise Rates to 2% by End-2027
item.description: The OECD forecasts Japan will raise its policy rate to 2% from 0.75% by end-2027, as robust domestic demand helps the economy absorb external shocks from the Middle East conflict.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳 Trumps China Summit Ambitions Shrink from Grand Bargain to Plea
item.description: President Trumps ambitions for his summit with Xi Jinping have shrunk from a grand bargain to a plea for help on Iran, as tech rivalry and distrust sap hopes for substantive AI commitments.

item.title: 🇻🇳🛢️ Vietnam seeks US approval for oil tanker to bypass Hormuz blockade
item.description: Vietnam has asked the US to allow an "extremely important" oil tanker carrying nearly 2 million barrels to pass through the Strait of Hormuz blockade. The ship is destined for Nghi Son refinery, which can process 200,000 barrels per day.

item.title: 🇱🇧⚔️ Israel strikes Lebanese highway, killing 8 including 2 children
item.description: Israeli airstrikes targeted cars on a highway south of Beirut, killing at least eight people including two children. The strikes come amid ongoing clashes between Israel and Hezbollah along the Lebanon border.

item.title: 🇦🇪🇱🇧 UAE designates 21 Lebanese citizens and groups as terrorists over Hezbollah ties
item.description: The United Arab Emirates has branded 21 Lebanese individuals and organizations as terrorists, ordering asset freezes and accusing them of connections to Hezbollah.

item.title: 🇸🇦 Saudi Arabia Conducted Covert Strikes on Iran During Regional War
item.description: Saudi Arabia launched multiple unpublicized attacks on Iranian soil in retaliation for strikes on the kingdom, marking the first known direct Saudi military action against Iran. The covert operations reveal how the Iran war has drawn in regional powers beyond the US and Israel.
